Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant Eli Lilly & Company To Provide Further Responses To First Set Of Interrogatories
Off Calendar Dec-05-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel a further response to special interrogatory #17 is granted. The interrogatory is redrafted by striking the following: "Do not obfuscate the record with supposed FDA compliance, state of the art, or what anyone else did, nor refer to irrelevant recipient studies. We want controlled daughter offspring studies only."

The objections are overruled and stricken. In addition, the general objections are stricken. There is no provision in the Code of Civil Procedure for general objections. Defendant is ordered to serve an answer that responds to the specific question posed by the interrogatory as re-drafted by the Court within 10 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff is entitled to a complete and straightforward response. Other than the phrase, ". . .it did not perform the testing which is the subject of this interrogatory," the balance of the response is evasive and non-responsive and should be stricken.

All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
